intro: Gonolobus bicolor, also known as the yellow and white milktongue vine, is a perennial plant species in the family Apocynaceae, native to tropical regions in Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with digestive issues and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Gonolobus bicolor include alkaloids, saponins, and flavonoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Gonolobus bicolor is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for wound healing and skin conditions.
